title: FAI Manifest Schema
description: The FAI Protocol glue file — a single JSON that wires all primitives together for a solution play. Describes context, primitive connections, quality gates, infrastructure, and toolkit. This is what makes FrootAI unique: no competitor has a context-wiring protocol.

knowledge.description: FROOT knowledge module IDs this play depends on (e.g., 'R2-RAG-Architecture', 'O3-MCP-Tools'). The FAI Engine resolves these to actual knowledge content.

waf.description: WAF pillars enforced for this play. All primitives must respect these guardrails.

scope.description: Scenario scope identifier (e.g., 'enterprise-rag-qa', 'multi-agent-orchestration'). Used for context isolation.

context.description: Shared knowledge context — which FROOT modules, WAF pillars, and scenario scope apply to all primitives.

agents.description: Paths to agent .md files (relative to play root).

groundedness.description: Minimum groundedness score (0-1). Recommended: 0.95 for enterprise.

safety.description: Maximum allowed safety violations. Must be 0 for production.

costPerQuery.description: Maximum cost per query in USD (e.g., 0.01). Enforced by WAF Cost pillar.

guardrails.description: Quality gates the FAI Engine evaluates after every agent response. Failures trigger retry or escalation.

primitives.description: All primitives wired into this play — agents, instructions, skills, hooks, workflows, and quality guardrails.

infrastructure.description: Infrastructure-as-code bindings. The FAI Engine validates and deploys these.

devkit.description: Path to DevKit folder (agents, instructions, prompts, skills, hooks).

tunekit.description: Path to TuneKit folder (config, evaluation, guardrails).

speckit.description: Path to SpecKit folder (architecture, waf-alignment, this manifest).

toolkit.description: FAI Toolkit paths — the three kits that equip every play.
